name: siuba (dplyr-style Data Manipulation for Python)

auth.notes: Local library; wraps pandas and SQLAlchemy

performance.notes: Thin wrapper over pandas/SQLAlchemy; overhead minimal vs underlying engine

agent_readiness.notes: R dplyr-style data manipulation for Python. from siuba import _, filter, select, mutate, summarize, group_by, arrange, count. from siuba import pipe as >>. mtcars >> filter(_.cyl == 6) >> select(_.mpg, _.cyl) >> arrange(-_.mpg). Siu expression: _['col'], _.col.str.upper(), _.col > 0. SQL backend: from siuba.sql import LazyTbl; tbl = LazyTbl(engine, 'table'). tbl >> filter(_.x > 0) >> collect(). Works on pandas DataFrames or SQLAlchemy tables with same syntax. group_by + summarize: mtcars >> group_by(_.cyl) >> summarize(avg_mpg=_.mpg.mean()). count(_.cyl). spread/gather for reshape. bind_rows, bind_cols.

gotchas: Niche library — small community and limited support resources
dplyr syntax is natural for R users but may feel foreign to Python-native developers
SQL backend (LazyTbl) has limited operation support vs pandas
No type annotations — limited IDE support
Less active development than dplyr.py or ibis-framework
best_when: R users transitioning to Python who prefer dplyr-style data manipulation
When pipe-style data manipulation (>>) is preferred over pandas chaining
Unified syntax across pandas DataFrames and SQL databases
avoid_when: Python-native teams unfamiliar with dplyr idioms
When pandas method chaining or polars is sufficient
summary: siuba brings R's dplyr syntax to Python. mtcars >> filter(_.cyl == 6) >> select(_.mpg) >> arrange(-_.mpg). Works on pandas and SQL (LazyTbl). group_by + summarize. MIT by Michael Chow. Best for R-to-Python data science transitions. Niche library — small community. No type annotations. dplyr idioms unfamiliar to Python-native teams. ibis-framework more mature for SQL-Python unification.
